derivation: Panel scores: worldgen 34, player 35, rendering 35, systems 36, perf 35. Median = 35, which is also the mode (three independent domains landed on 35 with medium-high confidence each). The two outliers are symmetric lens effects on the same facts, not new evidence: systems' 36 prices the five newly banked families (inventory 2, tools 4, hunger 2, health 2, save/load 2 — roughly 12-13 of 40 family weights vs ~4 last round) most generously while attributing the crafting fail narrowly; worldgen's 34 reflects its own stagnant domain (coherent-regions fail carried, structures at zero for a third round, floating-islet regression) rather than build-wide state, and 8 of its 29 items are still unverified-coded-only, so it is the discounted outlier per the merge rule. Trimming both toward the mode gives 35. Delta vs round 2's 33 = +2, decomposing as: +~3.5 from live-verified family weight and checklist conversions (soil column core, AO corners, underwater tint, break particles, crack stages, pause-freeze, jump/sprint/sneak kinetics with exact numbers, save/load round-trip, stability exoneration with zero console errors); -~1.5 for the single probe-proven fail, which is load-bearing — exact-count recipe matching plus no stack splitting makes every pick recipe unreachable in normal play, gutting the weight-4 crafting family and orphaning the otherwise fully verified tool stack behind a test hook. Held at 35 rather than 36 by harsh ties-down: the pointer-lock cluster (~5 cores plus wheel) is dark for a third consecutive round, 24/40 systems family weights (mobs, smelting, farming, light, weather, structures, redstone) are grep-proven absent, and the 50-band anchor (basic mobs) is untouched.

probe.observations: ENVIRONMENT FIRST (judges should know): the preview page spends most of its life with document.visibilityState=hidden, so rAF stops and the sim freezes between tool calls; screenshots pump ~0.5s of frames. getState().fps is only meaningful while frames flow: it read a solid 120 whenever the window was actually rendering (fresh load, mid-particle-storm screenshot), matching round 2, but decays to 0 between pumps — I could not get a clean post-teleport or under-load FPS number this round; nothing suggested a regression from 120. THE SPONTANEOUS RELOAD MYSTERY IS SOLVED AND THE GAME IS EXONERATED: the page reloaded out from under me 2-3 times (inventory/world resets mid-probe, 88 stacked three.js load warnings in the console buffer), but the game file (game/index.html) contains zero reload machinery (no location.reload, no WebSocket/EventSource); the reloads come from the Claude Preview harness (it also killed/restarted the python server once mid-session). Every reboot was clean: help overlay, 'Generating world… N%', deterministic spawn, zero console errors all session (only the three.js r150 deprecation warning per load). Probe traps for next round: timed-out preview_evals keep running as ZOMBIES inside the page (mine kept teleporting the player and breaking leaves on every later frame pump — make every eval self-contained and idempotent); setTimeout is intensively throttled while hidden (use MessageChannel hops); my MessageChannel rAF-spin shim eventually wedged the renderer hard enough to kill the preview server — do not background-pump, just screenshot to advance time. HUD text (pos/FPS) is often one frame stale. GAME FEEL: genuinely alive — adaptive crosshair, drumstick hunger bar with half-states, bubble meter only when submerged, color-coded durability bar, FLY tag, leaf-storm particles, item drops rendered with real textures (the spinning apple cube with red apples is a delight), 'Generating world' progress on boot, pause overlay doubling as the pointer-lock-lost screen. THE ONE REAL DESIGN BUG (verified_fail): the crafting UI matches recipes by EXACT grid counts and offers no stack splitting (right-click = left-click; shift-click moves whole stacks). Planks/logs can be shed by placing blocks to reach exact counts, but STICKS craft 4-at-a-time and cannot be placed, dropped, or split — so the exactly-2-sticks pick recipes are mathematically unreachable through the UI in normal play. I proved it empirically (3 planks + 4 sticks in grid -> blank output canvas, output click yields nothing) and only obtained a pick via the test hook craft(), which checks >= counts instead. This guts the survival tool progression loop despite every underlying tool system (tiers, durability, speed multipliers, drop gating) working beautifully. Minor oddities: two small floating grass islets (terrain blobs with flowers on top) in the desert at ~(560,115) — 3D-noise artifact, borderline for 'nothing floats midair'; one pause-interrupted mine appeared to finish early but reconstructs cleanly as a harness reload wiping the placed block (not a game bug); player death mid-probe was legitimate survival pressure (starvation at hunger 0 + incidental damage) and respawn restored spawn/vitals while KEEPING inventory. Sound verification is event-level only (headless): the game logs every sfx trigger to __vox.sfx — distinct break/place/step/bird-chirp/eat events all fired at the right moments; audibility and the M mute key untested. Recipes found: 1 log->4 planks, 2 planks->4 sticks, 4 planks->crafting table (3x3 UI exists via tableMode), 3 planks+2 sticks->wood pick (dur 60), 3 stone+2 sticks->stone pick (132), 3 iron ore+2 sticks->iron pick (251); hardness stone 2.2s/coal 2.8/iron 3.5/log 0.8; pick speed multipliers x0.32/0.25/0.18 gated to blocks with tool requirements.

top_gaps.title: Fix floating terrain caps from near-surface cave carving
top_gaps.detail: The probe found two floating grass islets with flowers on top at ~(560,115): cave() tunnels carve right up to y=h-1 (the depth-scaled threshold t=.052*min(1,.3+d/7) is nonzero at d=1, index.html:240-242), so a tunnel grazing the surface detaches the top block. Gate the carve on d>=3, or post-check that d[base+h] has support. Cheap, surgical, restores the vegetation-rules stretch I failed this round and protects the seamless-terrain cores from harsher scrutiny as probes roam wider.

panel.summary: Round 2's three lanes were systems-only by design, so worldgen code is byte-identical this round and my domain moved on evidence alone: net +1 point, with the soil-layering core finally converted (grass y28 -> dirt -> stone y24 under spawn) but the vegetation-rules stretch regressing to fail on a real discovery, two floating grass islets with flowers on top in the desert, a near-surface cave-carve artifact that violates 'nothing floats midair'. Determinism is now the domain's strongest pillar: four identical spawns, save-edits layered as diffs over regeneration, and clearSave restoring the exact pre-edit blocks, which also banks the worldgen-coupled save/load family. The whole build steps from the panel's 33 to 34 through my lens: the verified tools/hunger/save-load families (~8 family weights) and a raft of checklist conversions are real, but the crafting UI's unreachable tool recipes gut the survival loop the 50-band asks for, and harsh ties-down pricing caps the delta at +1. Worldgen is now the stagnant domain: coherent biome regions fail for a second round, structures sit at zero for a third, and mountains, snow line, rivers, ore depth, and foliage tint remain coded-but-never-observed, roughly 2.5 MPS of inventory the next round could harvest with mostly low-risk lanes.

top_gaps.title: Fix crafting reachability: >=-count recipe matching plus stack splitting

panel.summary: Round 3 turned my domain from a verified break/place substrate into a near-complete survival interaction layer: the kinetic cluster finally has live numbers (jump vy0=8.4 peaking +1.50 blocks, sprint with FOV kick and hunger gating both ways, sneak edge guard frozen at a ledge lip, hold-to-mine at per-block hardness with pick multipliers), and the inventory, tools, hunger, and health families all pass on faithful Minecraft math with zero regressions — the spontaneous-reload scare was exonerated as harness behavior. The round's one verified failure is band-defining: exact-count recipe matching plus no stack splitting makes the pick recipes unreachable in normal play, gutting the tool progression loop that the otherwise fully verified tool stack (tiers, durability, drop gating) deserves. Persistent silent debt remains in the pointer-lock cluster — real lock cycle, mouse look, A/D strafe, wall/ceiling collision, scroll wheel, and swim-up are unverified for a third consecutive round, roughly five cores of unconverted points. Against round 2's merged 33 I read the delta as +2: roughly +1.5 from newly banked family weight (tools, hunger, completed inventory, plus the kept-inventory respawn loop), +1.5 from kinetics verification cash-in, minus ~1 for the broken crafting reachability, landing at 35 with ties broken downward. The cheapest next-round wins are the one-evening crafting fix and two pure-verification lanes; the band-50 climb then hinges on a first mob.

top_gaps.title: Fix crafting reachability: >=-count matching plus stack splitting
top_gaps.detail: The UI matcher (index.html:781) demands exact grid counts while sticks craft 4-at-a-time and cannot be placed, dropped, or split, so all three pick recipes are unreachable in normal play — the single verified_fail this round and the cork in the entire tool-progression bottle. panel.summary: Round 3 is the round the survival systems became real: all five round-2 families are live-verified, not stubs — a 36-slot stacking inventory with a working drag/shift-click UI, tools whose timings match their hardness tables to the decimal (2.22s bare vs 0.72s picked stone) with durability that persists through save/load, a hunger system that actually gates sprint and kills via starvation, apples closing the food loop, and an auto-loading save that round-trips edits, vitals, and tool wear. The one hard fail is load-bearing: the exact-count recipe matcher plus zero stack splitting makes every pick recipe mathematically unreachable in normal play (proven empirically and structurally), so the otherwise-excellent tool tier system is orphaned behind a test hook and the crafting family fails harsh despite working mechanics — galling because the correct >=-semantics already exist in the file's own craft() hook. Everything past that wall is still provably absent: mobs, combat, smelting, farming, light propagation, weather, structures, and redstone account for 24 of 40 family weights at zero, and the 50-band anchor is unreachable without at least basic mobs. I place the build at 36, a +3 delta over round 2's merged 33: roughly +4.5 earned from the five newly banked families and the checklist conversions (soil column, hold-to-mine, crack stages, hunger coupling), minus ~1.5 for the crafting-reachability fail, with ties broken downward. Next round's highest-leverage order is the cheap crafting fix first (it converts already-built weight), then the mob lanes that define the next band.

top_gaps.title: Fix UI recipe matching to >=-and-consume plus stack splitting
top_gaps.detail: matchRecipe (game/index.html:776-783) requires exact grid counts and genClick offers no split (right-click = left-click), so the exactly-2-sticks pick recipes are unreachable in normal play — the round's only verified_fail, and it guts the survival progression loop the entire round-2 build was for. The craft() test hook (line 883) already implements the correct >=-consume semantics; mirror it in matchRecipe/outClick and add right-click-takes-half / right-click-places-one in genClick. Flips the crafting family from harsh-fail and secures the tools family's acquisition path.

top_gaps.title: Headed-session verification of the pointer-lock and audio cluster
top_gaps.detail: Pointer lock with re-lock, mouse look, wheel hotbar, context-menu suppression, z-fight motion check, and audio audibility plus the M mute key remain headless-blind after three rounds — roughly 6 core/stretch items (~10 checklist points) that one human or headed-browser pass converts for zero code risk. The lock() .catch hardening landed but has never been exercised live.

top_gaps.title: Entity substrate before mobs land
top_gaps.detail: Mobs are the largest remaining systems prize (8 weights across passive/hostile/light-linked) and the build now has three parallel gravity/collision implementations: player (physics/coll), drops (updDrops, lines 628-639), particles (updParts). Extract a shared fixed-tick entity struct with AABB helpers while only drops exist — adding mobs as a fourth bespoke updater multiplies both the bug surface and per-frame cost.

top_gaps.title: Chunk-data eviction using the now-existing edits diff
top_gaps.detail: The chunks Map (line 243) still grows ~16KB per visited chunk forever — carried from round 2 — but the blocker is now solved: edits/editB (line 432) already reconstruct player edits per chunk at genChunk (line 283). Evict pristine far Uint8Arrays alongside dropMesh; revisited terrain stays correct by regeneration plus diff re-apply, which the save/load probe just proved works.

top_gaps.title: CCmap scoped eviction instead of full clear()
top_gaps.detail: CI() still nukes all 400k cached column structs at the threshold (line 237), forcing a 10-octave-fbm recompute storm across every loaded chunk mid-trek. Carried unaddressed from round 2; keyed-by-chunk eviction removes the cliff before any mob AI starts querying CI per tick.

top_gaps.title: Pooled remesh scratch buffers
top_gaps.detail: buildChunk (lines 365-419) still allocates fresh JS arrays and recreates GPU buffers per remesh, with up to 3 chunk rebuilds per edit. Verified non-biting at 120 FPS, but lighting and mobs will multiply rebuild frequency; reusable typed-array scratch buffers cut GC bursts before they appear as hitching regressions. Carried from round 2.

top_gaps.title: Autosave serialization cost and quota visibility
top_gaps.detail: In normal mode save() (line 443) JSON.stringifies the entire edits Map every 15s on the main thread; cost grows linearly with lifetime edit count and quota failures are silently swallowed by the try/catch (invisible data loss). Debounce on edit-count delta, and surface a small HUD warning when localStorage.setItem throws.

top_gaps.title: Frame-guard granularity and idempotent test hooks
top_gaps.detail: loop()'s single try/catch (lines 855-869) warns once then swallows forever: a persistent throw in physics silently freezes sim AND render (renderer.render is inside the same try). Split sim/render guards and reset frameErr periodically. Also make __vox eval patterns idempotent or add a __vox.reset() — the probe's timed-out zombie evals kept teleporting the player and breaking blocks on every later frame pump, which will contaminate future probe evidence.

panel.summary: Through the perf/stability lens this is the strongest round yet: zero console errors and zero genuine crashes across the entire session (the spontaneous reloads are conclusively harness-side — the file contains no reload machinery), the 170-op stress burst left __vox.errors empty, and the FPS-60 scare is resolved as environmental — the loop is diff-verified structurally identical to round 1, round 2 added no meaningful per-frame cost, and the probe read 120 whenever frames actually flowed. Round 2's engineering quality holds up under inspection: the save system is an O(1) edit-diff (which incidentally solves the blocker on chunk eviction), drops stay memory-bounded, mineTick runs its own clamped clock, and the lock() .catch, frame guard, and drop terminal-velocity fixes all landed as specced. The deductions are a design bug, not a perf bug: exact-count recipe matching with no stack splitting makes tool crafting unreachable in normal play, failing the weight-4 crafting family harshly even though the tools family beneath it (hardness, tiers, durability, cracks) verified beautifully; and the carried debts — unbounded chunk Map, CCmap clear() cliff, per-edit remesh allocations, no entity substrate ahead of mobs — went a second round untouched. Delta since the round-2 panel's 33: +2 to 35, decomposing as roughly +3.5 from the newly banked families (hunger, save/load, completed inventory, tools mechanics, partial crafting) minus the crafting-loop fail, the audio-audibility hole, and the still-unverified pointer-lock cores; ties broken downward.

lanes.name: Lane 1 — Crafting reachability fix + input evidence hooks
lanes.goal: Fix the round's only probe-proven fail (pick recipes mathematically unreachable in normal play) and convert the headless-blind input cores by adding TEST-mode look/wheel paths. Expected ~+2.5 MPS (crafting family ~1.5, pointer-lock-cluster conversions ~1.0, survival QoL ~0.4 partial), all low risk.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. (1) RECIPE MATCHING: in matchRecipe() (lines 776-783), keep the key-set-size equality check at line 780 (no extraneous item TYPES may match) but change line 781 from `if(t[k]!==r.in[k])continue outer;` to `if((t[k]||0)<r.in[k])continue outer;` — i.e. >=-count semantics, mirroring the __vox.craft hook at line 883. outClick() (807-814) already consumes exactly r.in amounts and leaves remainders in the grid; verify and do not change its consume loop. Result: 3 planks + 4 sticks in the 2x2 grid must show Wood Pick in the output and crafting must leave 2 sticks behind. (2) STACK SPLITTING: thread the MouseEvent into genClick — change signature to genClick(arr,i,e) and pass e from slotClickI (799-802) and slotClickC (803-806). On e.button===2 with non-tool stacks: cur empty + slot filled -> cur takes Math.ceil(count/2), slot keeps the floor (null if 0); cur filled + slot empty -> place exactly 1; cur filled + same id + count<64 -> place 1 into slot; different ids -> swap as today. Tools (isTool) always move whole. Left-click behavior unchanged. contextmenu is already suppressed document-wide (line 512) so right-mousedown reaches the .islot handlers. (3) EAT-ANYWHERE: restructure tryPlace() (572-583): after the h.b===17 table branch (which still needs the ray hit), check `const it=inv[slot]; if(it&&it.id>=100){if(FOOD[it.id])eatFood(slot);return;}` BEFORE the placement-cell/overlap checks and before bailing on a null ray — so eating works aiming at the sky. Generalize eatApple (834-838) into eatFood(i) driven by a new table `const FOOD={105:4}` (restore FOOD[s.id], keep the 400ms eatT cooldown, hunger>=20 guard, and 'eat' logS). Update __vox.eat (line 901) to find any slot whose id is in FOOD. (4) Q DROP KEY: in the keydown handler (530-538) add `KeyQ` gated on (locked||TEST)&&!uiOpen&&!dead: remove 1 from inv[slot] (tools drop their whole stack carrying dur) and spawn a drop ~2.4 blocks in front of the eye along camera.getWorldDirection. Extend the drop struct/spawnDrop (622-627) to carry an optional dur and pass it through pickup (addItem at line 636 already honors st.dur per line 201). Add a pickup-immunity window in updDrops: skip the dd<1.1 pickup and dd<2.2 magnet branches while D.t<0.8 (prevents instant re-collection; existing break-drops gain a Minecraft-like sub-second delay, acceptable). (5) TEST LOOK/WHEEL: extract the mousemove body (520-522) into applyLook(mx,my) (yaw-=mx*.0022; pitch-=my*.0022; clamp +-1.55); mousemove calls it when locked. Add `__vox.look=(dx,dy)=>applyLook(dx,dy)`. Change the wheel gate at line 529 from `if(locked)` to `if(locked||TEST)`. Add `yaw` and `pitch` fields to __vox.getState() (line 875). Do NOT touch pointerlockchange/lock(), physics(), mineTick's block path, mousedown, worldgen, or the mesher. No new per-frame cost; all __vox additions are additive.
lanes.acceptance: All under ?test=1 with zero entries in __vox.errors and no console errors. (a) Crafting: __vox.give(16,3); __vox.give(100,4); __vox.toggleInv(false); move all 3 planks and all 4 sticks into the 2x2 grid via real mousedown events on .islot elements; the output slot canvas renders the Wood Pick sprite (non-zero alpha pixels); mousedown on output then close -> invFull() contains {id:101,dur:60}, exactly 3 planks and 2 sticks consumed, 2 sticks remain. (b) Split: with a 5-count stack in a slot, dispatch mousedown {button:2} on it -> cursor holds 3, slot keeps 2; right-mousedown on an empty slot places exactly 1. (c) Eat-anywhere: setCam aiming at empty sky (pitch +1.0), setHunger(10), select an apple slot, call __vox.place() -> getState().hunger===14 and apple count decremented. (d) Q-drop: select a stone stack of N, __vox.key('q') -> dropList() gains {id:3} ~2-3 blocks ahead and hotbar shows N-1; after ~1.5s of pumped frames walking near it, count returns to N. (e) Look: record getState().yaw, call __vox.look(100,0) -> yaw decreases by ~0.22; __vox.look(0,-10000) -> getState().pitch===1.55 exactly (clamp). (f) Wheel: dispatchEvent(new WheelEvent('wheel',{deltaY:120})) while unlocked in TEST -> getState().selected becomes the next slot's id; digit keys 1-9 still work. (g) Regressions: __vox.craft(101) still succeeds; closeInv returns grid contents to inventory; a full open/craft/close cycle leaves fps at 120 in a screenshot.

lanes.name: Lane 2 — First mobs: pig + zombie on a shared entity layer
lanes.goal: Bank the two heaviest absent systems families (passive mobs weight 3, hostile mobs + combat weight 4) — the explicit 50-band anchor — with one shared entity substrate so a fourth bespoke physics path is not created. Expected ~+3.5-4 MPS. Highest-risk lane; isolated to a new module plus four one-line integration points.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. Add a new MOBS section directly after the drops section (after line 639). (1) SUBSTRATE: `let mobs=[]`, MOBMAX=8. Mob struct {type,x,y,z,vx,vy,vz,yaw,hp,t,hurtT,atkT,wanderT,grp}. One generic AABB resolver entColl(M,hw,hh,axis,amt) mirroring the player's coll() pattern (453-460) using getB/SOLID — do NOT modify coll() itself. Per-frame in updMobs(dt): gravity -25*dt clamped -45, axis-separated x/z then substepped y (0.9-block substeps like physics()), auto-jump vy=7.5 when a horizontal axis is blocked while grounded. Zero per-frame allocations: reuse module-level scratch vars. (2) TYPES: pig — hp 5, speed 1.4, hw .35/hh .9, wander AI (every 2-5s pick a random heading or idle via Math.random, transient state so determinism of worldgen/saves is untouched), flees at speed 3.2 for 3s after being hurt; on death drops item id 106. zombie — hp 10, speed 2.3, hw .3/hh 1.8, chases the player when within 24 blocks (heading = atan2 toward pos), contact attack: when its AABB comes within 1.0 of the player's, call the existing damage(2) (line 842) with a 1.0s per-mob cooldown; despawns in daylight. (3) RENDERING: per-type THREE.Group built from <=6 shared BoxGeometry boxes with one shared MeshBasicMaterial matMob whose .color is copied from matO.color at the top of updMobs (same day/night tint pattern as matDrop, line 628). Pink pig (body+head+4 leg stubs), green zombie (head+torso+2 arms+2 legs). Share geometries across instances; on despawn remove the group from scene only (no geometry dispose). Simple walk bob (group.position.y wobble) and yaw facing travel direction. (4) COMBAT INPUT: function attackMob(): step a ray from camera.position along camera.getWorldDirection in 0.25 increments to t=3.5, test point-in-AABB against each mob; nearest hit takes dmg = (inv[slot]&&PICKT[inv[slot].id])?3:2, knockback vx/vz = 6*normalized(player->mob) and vy=4, sets hurtT for a 0.3s red-tint flash (material color swap or scale pulse), returns true. Hook into the mousedown handler (525-527): on e.button===0, call attackMob() first; if it hit, do NOT begin mining this click (skip mL=true/resetMine for this event). On mob death: burst() particles at its position, pig spawns the 106 drop via spawnDrop. (5) NEW ITEM 106 Porkchop: NAMES[106]='Porkchop' (line 186), ITILE[106]=31 (line 192), draw T(31,...) meat sprite in the free atlas tile, and add `106:8` to lane 1's FOOD table so eating restores 8 hunger. (6) SPAWNER: once per ~1s inside updMobs: skip entirely when TEST unless spawnOn (default false; toggled by __vox.setSpawning) so plain ?test=1 stays pristine and probe-deterministic. Track daylight via a module global gDL assigned from dl inside updSky (one line at ~699). Night (gDL<.12): if zombies<4, try a random angle/dist 18-32 from player, require CI(x,z).h>SEA and not water, spawn at h+1. Day: pigs<4 on grass columns (tp===1) 16-32 away. All mobs despawn beyond 56 blocks; zombies despawn within ~2s once gDL>.5 (with a particle burst). Mobs are never saved (transient across reloads). (7) INTEGRATION: add updMobs(dt) inside the `if(active&&!dead)` branch of loop() (line 860) after updDrops(dt) — this automatically freezes mobs on pause/death. (8) __vox additions: spawnMob(type,x,y,z) (y null -> CI.h+1, returns true/false at cap), mobs:()=>mobs.map(M=>({type:M.type,x:M.x,y:M.y,z:M.z,hp:M.hp})), clearMobs(), setSpawning(b), attack:()=>attackMob(). Do NOT touch physics(), coll(), worldgen, the mesher, save(), or the crafting UI.
lanes.acceptance: All under ?test=1, __vox.errors empty throughout. (a) Chase: __vox.setTime(0); __vox.spawnMob('zombie', px+10, null, pz); pump ~120 frames -> __vox.mobs()[0] horizontal distance to player monotonically shrinks below 2.5; it never falls through the floor (y stays within terrain) and climbs a 1-block step placed in its path. (b) Contact damage: stand still adjacent -> getState().hp drops by 2 per ~1s tick, dmg flash fires, and at hp 0 the existing death overlay appears. (c) Combat: setCam facing the zombie at ~2 blocks; __vox.attack() -> mobs()[0].hp drops (2 bare, 3 holding a pick) and its position is displaced >=0.4 away from the player; repeated attacks kill it -> mobs() empty plus a particle burst observed in screenshot. (d) Pig loop: spawnMob('pig') -> over ~600 pumped frames its position changes >2 blocks while staying grounded; 3 bare attacks kill it; dropList() gains {id:106}; walking over it adds Porkchop to inventory; setHunger(8) then __vox.eat() with it selected -> hunger 16. (e) Despawn: with a zombie alive, setTime(12) -> zombies gone from mobs() within ~3s of pumped frames; mobs beyond 56 blocks get culled. (f) Spawner: setSpawning(true); setTime(0); pump ~30s -> at least one zombie in mobs() within 40 blocks; setSpawning(false); clearMobs() -> stays empty. (g) Stability/determinism: plain ?test=1 boot spawns zero mobs and the spawn position/terrain are byte-identical to pre-lane values for seed 1337; pause() freezes all mobs() coordinates bit-identical across 2s; screenshot with 6 mobs active reads FPS 120; 170-op stress burst plus mobs leaves zero console errors.

lanes.name: Lane 3 — Worldgen repair + seeded micro-structures
lanes.goal: Fix the floating-cap regression and the carried biome/beach incoherence fails, make mountains/snow-line findable, and bank the first villages/structures-family evidence (weight 3, at zero for three rounds) — all in CI/genChunk only, never touching the mesher. Expected ~+1.8-2.2 MPS, low-medium risk.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html, confined to the worldgen section (lines 210-290) plus additive __vox helpers. Use ONLY the seeded hashes h2i/h3i/h2f/fbm2 — no Math.random anywhere in generation, so seeded determinism is preserved exactly (terrain WILL re-baseline for seed 1337: spawn coords shift; that is expected and fine since the spawn search at 447-451 is itself deterministic). (1) FLOATING-CAP FIX: in genChunk's column loop, change line 255 from `if(cOK&&y>1&&cave(wx,y,wz,h))b=0;` to gate the carve at least 3 below the surface: `if(cOK&&y>1&&h-y>=3&&cave(wx,y,wz,h))b=0;`. This makes the (560,115)-style detached grass islets impossible. Leave cave() itself (238-242) and the tree-placement cave check (line 265) untouched. (2) BEACH CLAMP: extract the height computation of CI (the cont/amp/rv stack, lines 214-221) into a helper rawH(x,z) that returns only h (no CCmap write, no biome/veg work) so neighbor lookups cannot recurse. Replace the line-225 rule `if(h<=SEA+1){tp=4;un=4;}` with: sand iff h<=SEA (submerged/at-sea) OR (h<=SEA+2 AND any of rawH(x±3,z), rawH(x,z±3) < SEA). Low-lying plains far from water now fall through to their biome surface (grass). (3) BIOME DE-JITTER: the line-222 label currently flips per-column at hard t/m thresholds. Compute the biome label from lower-octave fields — e.g. tB=fbm2(x*.0045+813.7,z*.0045-217.3,2) and mB likewise with 2 octaves — used ONLY for the biome string and vegetation/tree-type choices, keeping the 3-octave t/m for tint blending (226-229). Goal: no single-column biome islands. (4) FINDABLE MOUNTAINS + SNOW LINE: widen the mountain window at line 216 from sst(.55,.85,cont) to sst(.48,.78,cont) and raise the amp coefficient at 217 from 38*mtn to ~46*mtn. Builder MUST verify empirically (quick CI scan in console) that for seed 1337 some column within 500 blocks of spawn reaches h>=46, so the h>=45 snow cap (line 223) and the h>=42 stone band (line 225 chain) become observable; tune the two constants until true. Confirm the spawn search still lands on a safe flat column. (5) SEEDED MICRO-STRUCTURES: add a structure pass in genChunk after tree decoration (after line 282) and BEFORE the editB re-apply at 283 so player edits layer over structures. Decision: if h2i(cx^0xA5A5,cz^0x5A5A)%37===0, candidate at chunk-local (8,8); require flatness (max-min CI.h over the 5x5 around it <=1), h>SEA+1, no tree/cactus at center. Desert biome -> well: 3x3 stone (id 3) rim at h with a 1-deep water (id 5) center cell and 4 corner stone posts at h+1. Other land biomes -> ruin: a 4x4 broken stone-wall square, per-cell height 0-2 chosen by h3i(wx,0,wz)%3 (gaps included), with one crafting table (id 17) inside as loot flavor. Keep every structure cell within chunk-local 2..13 so no cross-chunk writes are needed; write via direct d[] index or tset(...,force). Everything keyed off h2i/h3i only. (6) __vox HELPERS (additive, inside the TEST block at 872-903): colInfo:(x,z)=>{const c=CI(x,z);return{h:c.h,biome:c.biome,tp:c.tp,snow:c.snow}}, and findStructures:(r)=>scan chunk coords within radius r of spawn using the same hash + flatness predicate (pure, no chunk generation) returning [{type,x,y,z}]. Optionally widen the river window at line 220 from sst(.05,.015,rv) to sst(.06,.02,rv) as a secondary item. Do NOT touch buildChunk/the mesher, physics, UI, audio, or the loop.
lanes.acceptance: All under ?test=1, __vox.errors empty. (a) No floating caps: sweep a 128x128 area (step 2) around spawn AND around a desert region: for every column where colInfo.tp is 1/3/4/8, assert NOT (block(x,h,z)!==0 && block(x,h-1,z)===0) — zero violations (round 3 found two at ~(560,115)-equivalents). (b) Beach clamp: sample >=200 columns with biome==='plains' and h>=SEA+3 -> zero have tp===4; sample >=10 shoreline columns (h===SEA+1 within 3 of a column with h<SEA) -> all tp===4; screenshot of a shoreline still shows a sand strip. (c) De-jitter: on a 64x64 step-1 grid spanning a biome border, zero columns whose biome differs from all four orthogonal neighbors (no single-column islands); HUD biome agrees with ground cover in a teleport screenshot. (d) Mountains/snow: scanning colInfo on a step-8 grid within 500 of spawn finds max h>=46; teleporting there shows a peak with stone band and white snow cap in the screenshot, HUD y confirms >=45 terrain. (e) Structures: __vox.findStructures(600) returns >=2 entries; teleport to one of each type -> screenshot shows the well (stone rim, water center via block()===5) or ruin (stone walls + crafting table via block()===17); clearSave()+reload returns the identical findStructures list and identical blocks (determinism). (f) Regressions: fresh boot spawns standing safe (not in water/cave/falling) at the same coords across two consecutive loads; clearSave+reload regenerates a probed dug block exactly; 400-block teleport still fully meshes in ~1s with no seams; zero console errors; FPS 120 in screenshots.

built.changes_summary: All edits in game/index.html, exactly per spec. (1) matchRecipe: count check changed from strict equality to >=-count semantics (`(t[k]||0)<r.in[k]`), key-set-size equality kept; outClick consume loop untouched — pick recipes now reachable in the 2x2 grid with remainders left behind. (2) genClick gained an `e` param with a right-click (button 2) branch for non-tool stacks: empty cursor takes ceil(n/2)/slot keeps floor; cursor over empty slot places exactly 1; same-id slot under 64 receives 1; different ids swap; tools fall through to whole-stack moves. slotClickI/slotClickC now pass `e`. (3) tryPlace restructured: crafting-table branch still requires the ray hit, then the inv[slot].id>=100 food/no-place check runs before the null-ray bail and placement checks, so eating works aiming at the sky. eatApple generalized to eatFood(i) driven by new `const FOOD={105:4}` (cooldown, hunger guard, 'eat' logS kept); __vox.eat now searches any FOOD id. (4) KeyQ in the keydown handler (gated (locked||TEST)&&!uiOpen&&!dead) drops 1 item (tools drop whole carrying dur) ~2.4 blocks along camera.getWorldDirection; spawnDrop carries optional dur, pickup passes D.dur into addItem; updDrops skips pickup/magnet branches while D.t<0.8. (5) mousemove body extracted into applyLook(mx,my) with the +-1.55 clamp; wheel gate widened to (locked||TEST); __vox.look added; yaw/pitch added to getState. Verified live in the preview under ?test=1 with manual frame pumping (hidden tab pauses rAF): (a) real-mousedown crafting of Wood Pick — sprite renders (40 alpha px), exactly 3 planks + 2 sticks consumed, 2 sticks remain, {id:101,dur:60} lands in inventory; (b) 5-stack right-click -> cursor 3/slot 2, right-click empty places exactly 1, totals conserved; (c) sky-aimed eat: hunger 10->14, apple decremented; (d) Q-drop spawns 2.40 blocks ahead, immunity window holds, re-collected to original count, tool drop preserves dur; (e) look(100,0) yaw -0.22, pitch clamps to exactly 1.55; (f) wheel changes selection while unlocked in TEST, digit keys still work; (g) __vox.craft(101) regression OK, closeInv returns grid contents, zero __vox.errors and no console errors throughout.
built.lane: Lane 1 — Crafting reachability fix + input evidence hooks
built.notes: Risks/follow-ups: (1) The preview page runs hidden, so requestAnimationFrame is fully paused — the world appears stuck at "Generating world... 1%" and physics-dependent probes stall unless frames are pumped (window.loop() is globally reachable; I pumped it manually). The harness must drive frames or run a visible/headless-with-vsync browser; this is environmental, not a code defect. (2) FPS measured 60 under my 16ms manual pump cadence, which only proves no per-frame regression at that cadence; the 120 FPS screenshot check needs the real harness. (3) Right-click split intentionally falls through to whole-stack semantics when either the cursor or the slot holds a tool, and shift-click still takes priority over button checks (pre-existing behavior). (4) The 0.8s pickup-immunity also delays block-break drop collection by under a second — accepted per spec as Minecraft-like. (5) updDrops' magnet branch is skipped during immunity, so a Q-dropped item cannot boomerang even when walking forward immediately. built.changes_summary:
Lane 2 complete — pig + zombie mobs on one shared entity substrate in game/index.html.

(1) MOBS section inserted directly after the drops section (now lines ~653-757, before the audio section): `let mobs=[]`, MOBMAX=8, module globals gDL/mobSpawnT/spawnOn, generic `entColl(M,hw,hh,axis,amt)` AABB resolver mirroring the player's coll() pattern (coll() itself untouched), and `updMobs(dt)` doing gravity -25*dt clamped -45, axis-separated x/z, 0.9-block substepped y, and auto-jump vy=7.5 when a horizontal axis is blocked while grounded. Zero per-frame allocations (module scratch wScx/wScz, numeric locals only).
(2) Types: pig (hp 5, spd 1.4, hw .35/hh .9, wander every 2-5s via transient Math.random, flees at 3.2 for 3s after hurt, drops item 106 on death) and zombie (hp 10, spd 2.3, hw .3/hh 1.8, chases player within 24 via atan2 heading, AABB-gap<1.0 contact attack calling existing damage(2) with 1.0s per-mob atkT cooldown, burns up ~1.5s after gDL>.5).
(3) Rendering: shared deduped BoxGeometries (pig: body/head/leg ×4 = pink; zombie: head/torso/arm ×2/leg ×2 = green) with baked per-face-shaded vertex colors; one shared matMob (vertexColors) whose .color copies matO.color at the top of updMobs (matDrop pattern) plus matMobH for the 0.3s hurt red-flash material swap; walk bob on group.position.y; yaw faces travel; despawn removes group only, no geometry dispose.
(4) Combat: `attackMob()` steps a camera ray in 0.25 increments to t=3.5 vs mob AABBs, nearest hit takes 2 dmg (3 with a pick via PICKT), knockback vx/vz=6*normalized(player→mob) + vy=4, 0.3s hurt stun; hooked into mousedown (line 531): left-click attacks first and skips mL=true/resetMine on hit. Death → burst() particles; pig death → spawnDrop(...,106).
(5) Item 106 Porkchop: NAMES (line 191), ITILE 106:31 (line 197), new T(31) meat sprite drawn before the avg[] computation so burst colors pick it up, FOOD 106:8 (line 950).
(6) Spawner: 1s cadence inside updMobs, fully skipped when TEST unless spawnOn (default false, via __vox.setSpawning) so plain ?test=1 stays pristine; gDL assigned from dl in updSky (line 810); night (gDL<.12) zombies<4 at dist 18-32 needing CI.h>SEA + non-solid cell; day (gDL>.5) pigs<4 on tp===1 grass 16-32; cull beyond 56 blocks; mobs never saved.
(7) Loop integration: updMobs(dt) appended inside the `if(active&&!dead)` branch after updDrops(dt) (line 977) — pause/death freezes mobs.
(8) __vox additions (after dropList): spawnMob(type,x,y,z) (y null → CI.h+1, false at cap), mobs(), clearMobs(), setSpawning(b), attack().

built.notes: Risks/follow-ups: (1) Acceptance (a) timing depends on harness frame dt — zombie speed is spec-locked at 2.3 blocks/s, so closing 10→2.5 blocks takes ~3.3s of simulated time; 120 frames suffices at dt≈0.028s+ (dt is clamped at .05 so slow pumps converge faster). Approach is monotonic since steering always targets the player. (2) Knockback displacement ≥0.4 is guaranteed by a 0.3s hurt-stun (AI velocity zeroed while hurtT>0, ~0.9 blocks net push); if the harness measures >1s after a single attack on a chasing zombie, it may have re-closed — repeated attacks per acceptance (c) re-knock each time. (3) Hurt flash is a shared-material swap (matMob→matMobH red tint) per group, not per-instance color mutation, keeping one day/night-tinted material as specced. (4) Zombies idle (no wander) outside 24-block aggro — spec only requires chase; cheap to add later. (5) Spawner runs by default in normal (non-?test=1) play, per spec gating only the TEST path. Static verification: node --check on the extracted script passes, file ends with </html>, no duplicate declarations, all 4 integration points are one-liners (mousedown line 531, gDL line 810, FOOD line 950, loop line 977). physics()/coll()/worldgen/mesher/save()/crafting UI untouched.

built.notes: Risks/follow-ups: (1) Terrain re-baselined for seed 1337 as expected — spawn is now (116,28,115); harness baselines referencing old coords must refresh. (2) Acceptance (b) shoreline check: clamp tests exactly ±3 orthogonal offsets per spec; my within-1..3 orthogonal sweep showed 84/84 sand, but a harness using diagonal adjacency could in principle find an uncaught column.
